name: David Ramirez
backstory: David just got promoted and needs professional headshot-quality photos for his LinkedIn and company bio. He bought a Sony a6400 six months ago after reading it was 'the best beginner camera' but it's been sitting in the box because the manual is 300 pages. He has maybe 2 hours per week to learn. He's very goal-driven — he does not want to become a photographer, he wants to take one great photo of himself. He's used to learning from structured, numbered steps.
emotional_triggers: Wasting time — if something doesn't feel productive in 10 minutes, he moves on
Feeling like a beginner in front of others (he's used to being the expert in the room)
Jargon without explanation — he'll disengage if he doesn't understand why something matters
existing_strengths: Highly goal-oriented — very clear on what success looks like
Patient and methodical when he understands the payoff
Already owns good equipment and is willing to invest time if the ROI is clear
goals: Get usable results in under an hour of practice
success_looks_like: David takes a photo he'd actually use on LinkedIn. He understands 2-3 settings he changed and why.
failure_looks_like: David gets taught the full exposure triangle before getting to take a single photo.
